data.content:
I can see the problem. The target generates the file but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file isn't automatically picked up during build execution.

The fix: add the generated file to `<Compile>` and register it with `<FileWrites>` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file `GeneratedInfo.cs` but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically. The target needs to explicitly add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>`.

Let me also use the skill for this since there's a relevant one.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's what was wrong and what I fixed:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never tells MSBuild to compile it. SDK-style implicit globs (`**/*.cs`) expand at **evaluation time** — before any targets run — so the file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target (after file generation) to:
- `<Compile Include="...">` — adds the file to the compilation
- `<FileWrites Include="...">` — registers it so `dotnet clean` removes it
